title: verbascum novae-hollandiae
intro: Verbascum novae-hollandiae, also known as the New Holland mullein, is a biennial herb native to Australia and New Zealand. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which can be used to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The leaves and flowers of Verbascum novae-hollandiae can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
